overview: The Nob Hill / Northwest District is a vibrant, walkable neighborhood in Portland, Oregon, characterized by historic residential fabric from the early-to-mid 20th century, an active commercial corridor along NW 23rd Avenue, strong transit access via multiple TriMet bus lines, and a mix of long-time residents and new arrivals with an active neighborhood association.
walkability_highlights: High destination density (shops, restaurants, services within walking distance)

key_streets.NW 23rd Avenue: Main commercial corridor with retail, dining, and services
key_streets.NW Thurman Street: Adjacent major arterial with transit connections

community_assets: Mature street tree canopy providing shade and aesthetic value
Community engagement through active neighborhood association
Local business community supporting independent commerce
data_source: OpenStreetMap via OSM MCP Server — © OpenStreetMap contributors, ODbL 1.0
